purpose: TaintSinkType match-site audit post-extension (4 ADDITIVE variants: HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize)
audit_methodology: Ran `cargo check --workspace` after adding 4 variants — clean build, no non-exhaustive match warnings.
Ran `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings` — clean, zero warnings.
Ran `grep -rn 'match .*sink_type\|match.*TaintSinkType\b' --include=*.rs crates/` to enumerate all match expressions on the enum.

matches_macro_sites.behavior: All sites use `matches!(x.sink_type, TaintSinkType::ShellExec)` filter form. The `matches!` macro is non-exhaustive by design — adding new variants does NOT trigger compiler errors. No extension required.

construction_sites.behavior: Sites of the form `sink_type: TaintSinkType::ShellExec` (struct literal construction) are not match sites — they construct one variant. No extension required.

false_positive_match_at_text_format.actual_pattern: let snk_label = match sink_type { Some(st) => ..., None => ... }
false_positive_match_at_text_format.note: Matches on Option<...>, not on TaintSinkType variants. No extension required.

validator_mandate_compliance.taintsinktype_extension_additive_only: PASS — 4 variants added in additive position after FileWrite. Existing 6 variants (SqlQuery, CodeEval, CodeExec, CodeCompile, ShellExec, FileWrite) preserved verbatim. No wildcard arms introduced (none required).

predicted_vs_actual.explanation: Codebase consistently uses `matches!()` filters and struct-literal construction rather than exhaustive `match` arms on TaintSinkType. The investigation prediction was conservative; the additive-only mandate was satisfied trivially.
